transport.path: ~/.tmux-worktree/feishu-bridge-v1.sock; SHA-256 HOME fallback under tmp when the UTF-8 path exceeds 100 bytes

capabilityNegotiation.rollingUpgrade: a Dashboard may reuse a daemon for new or resumed Feishu ownership only when the selected larkProfile and required capabilities match; an empty daemon missing required capabilities may be shut down and replaced; an occupied older daemon remains available for status, canonical removal, and controlled takeover, but create/resume/repair/return fail with FEISHU_BRIDGE_UPGRADE_REQUIRED when binding.structured-agent-result.v1, binding.steering.v1, bridge.consumer-health.v1, or reply.durable-payload.v1 is missing; confirmed stale/pausing removal may migrate a legacy daemon only when it is the daemon's sole binding and no active turn would be disrupted
capabilityNegotiation.legacyCreate: new binding creation requires binding.structured-agent-result.v1, binding.steering.v1, bridge.consumer-health.v1, and reply.durable-payload.v1; never send fields unsupported by an old closed binding.create parser

eventSource.dashboardConversationPolicy: empty allowedSenderIds means any human user in the bound group may trigger only by mentioning the exact bot; bot/self, non-user, and unmentioned messages are ignored
eventSource.health: initial daemon readiness requires the lark-cli event consumer child to spawn; bridge.snapshot exposes starting, running, or backoff with a bounded error; Dashboard disables new group links while the consumer is unavailable, while a later exit is retried without discarding existing bindings

reply.bindingMode: topic or direct; persisted per binding and changed only by the closed binding.update operation while no turn is active
reply.defaultMode: topic, including legacy version 1 binding records that omit options.replyMode
reply.threading: topic replies appear in the source message topic; direct replies preserve the source-message reply relationship but appear in the main chat; delivery never falls back from one configured mode to the other
reply.cardTitle: tw agent on <binding session name>; the context is normalized to one line and bounded to 48 Unicode characters, and never contains the full user prompt or terminal output
reply.rawMarkerRole: raw generation-fenced output is used only to detect a closed current-turn nonce and is never sent as card content
reply.controllerCapability: binding create/resume/repair/return must prove terminal-control advertises output.rendered-snapshot, activity.agent-status, and activity.agent-result before acquiring or accepting Feishu ownership; every authorized inbound turn repeats the rendered-snapshot proof before event dedup or terminal input so a rolled-back controller cannot receive a prompt that the Bridge cannot safely render
reply.renderedExtraction: after raw close detection, extract public text only from a bounded terminal-control rendered snapshot fenced by the exact Feishu lease, pane, control epoch, fence, and output generation; an incomplete snapshot never falls back to raw terminal text
reply.renderedObservationFailure: bounded resource exhaustion, controller-internal capture failure, and ordinary transport failure remain read-only observations and retry until the turn inactivity deadline; permission, target, generation, protocol, or correlation failures immediately make the turn recovery-required, never send Agent output, and enqueue a separate content-free recovery status card
reply.unknownAcknowledgement: the primary reply becomes uncertain and the turn becomes recovery-required; its Agent content is never blindly retried unless the exact fully persisted prepared payload is still pending under the same idempotency key, and a separate content-free recovery status card is enqueued
reply.durability: before the first Lark call, the Bridge atomically persists the exact bounded and sanitized Card payload, destination mode, terminal-authority-proven final turn state, and deterministic idempotency key; a daemon restart resumes a still-prepared payload with that same key, while legacy prepared records without the payload fail closed as uncertain

bindingLifecycleNotices.onCreate: sent after the exact binding and Feishu lease are durable
bindingLifecycleNotices.onManualRemove: sent after a live Feishu lease is released, or authority proves the target is FREE, TARGET_GONE, or held by a controlled local owner, and the binding is durably removed; missing lease plus Feishu/recovery ownership fails closed; current Dashboard and CLI callers identify their local removal origin for the audit card, while an omitted origin is reported only as an unknown local management client
bindingLifecycleNotices.legacyManualRemove: one-time removal of an active/paused binding through an already-running legacy daemon cannot emit this newer lifecycle card; Dashboard keeps that canonical legacy removal path to avoid interrupting its live authority, and later current-capability operations upgrade the empty daemon
bindingLifecycleNotices.onTargetGone: all persisted bindings, including paused and stale bindings, are reconciled by exact controlTargetId; only certain ended/replaced lifecycles are removed and announced
bindingLifecycleNotices.sameNameRecreation: never auto-retarget; announce replacement only after the old exact target is proven TARGET_GONE
bindingLifecycleNotices.uncertainTargetState: recovery, transport failure, or a new controlTargetId caused only by unknown controller continuity remains fail-closed and is not announced as deletion or replacement
bindingLifecycleNotices.delivery: best-effort ordered effect lane with deterministic idempotency; notification latency or failure never blocks lease renewal or rolls back terminal ownership or binding state

localTaskCompletion.scope: when Feishu first acquires or regains an exact binding lease, a lease-, generation-, and pane-fenced activity.agent-status observation arms a persisted watch only after Claude or Codex is observed running with an exact structured transcript source
localTaskCompletion.steeringPolicy: while an inherited local task watch is probing, armed, or stop-candidate, the first authorized group message is submitted through the canonical fenced input.agent-message operation as a start-or-steer prompt, cancels the inherited completion watch only after accepted input, and creates the one marker-correlated Feishu reply turn; a watch already sending its completion card rejects new input until that outbound disposition settles
localTaskCompletion.settlement: two stopped observations separated by at least one second complete the watch; a later running observation clears a stop candidate
localTaskCompletion.delivery: after earlier binding lifecycle effects settle, an independent outbound lane uses lark-cli im +messages-send to post one top-level Card JSON 2.0 final-answer card with a deterministic idempotency key; Feishu network latency never occupies the terminal mutation lane, and topic/direct reply placement does not apply because there is no source Feishu message
localTaskCompletion.content: activity.agent-result extracts only the exact final assistant text from the correlated Claude or Codex structured transcript. Terminal rendering, tool calls/results, composer content, status/footer text, and inferred answers are never eligible card content; a bounded long answer is explicitly marked truncated
localTaskCompletion.unknownAcknowledgement: persist uncertain, mark the binding stale, never retry Agent content blindly, and enqueue a separate content-free recovery status card
localTaskCompletion.restart: a sending watch becomes uncertain and enqueues a content-free recovery status card; an armed watch remains dormant while the binding is paused/stale and is re-correlated when controlled ownership returns to Feishu

reactions.success: remove Typing; the reply card is the success signal
reactions.deleteFailure: do not add a contradictory CrossMark
reactions.authority: best-effort UX only; reaction failures never change terminal ownership or reply disposition
reactions.durability: reaction handles are bounded in-memory state; a hard daemon crash or uncertain create/delete acknowledgement can leave Typing until manually removed

storage.bindingReplyModeMigration: a version 1 binding missing options.replyMode is read as topic and the next write persists it explicitly; sessionSummary and activityWatch are optional additive binding fields; durable outbound payload fields are optional only for reading legacy reply history and are required as one coherent closed group for every new prepared reply; unknown fields, unknown reply modes, and unknown activity watch states remain malformed state; after newer fields are written, an older strict daemon fails closed on downgrade instead of silently changing behavior

limits.activeTurnSteering: later authorized group messages use distinct deterministic input.agent-message operation IDs to steer the existing turn, reuse its marker/output correlation, and trigger a best-effort idempotent status acknowledgement; they never create a second reply turn, and the final answer remains attached to the first source message
limits.turnTimeoutCompatibility: turnTimeoutMs is retained as the v1 manifest compatibility key; it now denotes the same consecutive no-output interval as turnIdleTimeoutMs, not a total Agent runtime budget
limits.turnDeadlinePolicy: deadlineAt begins ten minutes after accepted input and slides ten minutes after every newly observed byte under the same control target, epoch, generation, and fence; polling performs one final fenced output observation before declaring inactivity
limits.completionPolicy: group-originated turns still require the exact closed public marker extracted from a correlated rendered snapshot. An inherited running-to-stopped watch may send only the exact activity.agent-result final assistant text from its persisted structured transcript source; terminal capture is never a fallback.
